meta.title: Experiences - Mouvement Research, Harvests, Bees, and Slow Days
meta.description: Farm breakfasts, Mouvement Research, kids playground, beekeeping, olive harvest, stargazing, garden work, and visits around Testour.

hero: There is always something to do. And always permission to do nothing.
items: Made every morning from what the farm produces. Eggs, oil, yogurt, fruit, bread, juice.
Mahdi's indoor and outdoor practice space for handstands, calisthenics, rings, climbing, mobility, strength and physical exploration.
A family-friendly play area with trampoline-style play and open farm space for children to move, climb, run and explore.
Walk the trails. Sit under the olive trees. Watch the animals. The land is yours to explore.
One of the farm's best-kept secrets. The rooftop at night, far from city light.
Yoga, calisthenics, mobility, handstand practice. Every morning, if you want it and Mahdi is available.
Suit up and visit the hives with Mahdi. Learn how the bees live and how the honey is made.
October to December - join the harvest, press your own oil, take a bottle home.
Pomegranates, figs, almonds - depending on the season, there is always something to pick.
Planting, watering, harvesting vegetables from the food garden. Hands in the soil.
Feed the chickens. Walk with the donkey. Spend time with the horse. The animals are daily life here.
Mahdi is always building something. If you want to help and learn, you are welcome.
Communal cooking around the open-air kitchen. Fire, food, conversation.
15 minutes away - pomegranates, Andalusian heritage, and the weekly market. Mahdi takes guests weekly.
